Home.title: A Luxurious Escape in the Heart of History
Home.heroTitle:
Elegant Aegean Experience
In a Premium Stone Mansion
Home.heroText: Within 500-year-old registered stone architecture, 12 km from the sea, personalized service and a refined escape.
Home.roomsText: Feel the texture of history in our soulfully designed rooms.
Home.conciergeText: Not just a stay; specially designed services for you to collect memories that last a lifetime.
Home.bookingText: Direct booking advantage with best price guarantee, flexible cancellation, and concierge support.
Home.faqText: Details that might cross your mind before booking.

experiences.title: Antakya & Aegean Gastronomy
experiences.text: İnci Hanım's special recipes, sac kavurma, and fresh coffee ground in a 500-year-old stone dibek.

experiences.title: VIP Transfer & Fast Check-in
experiences.text: Airport transfer and direct room entry privilege.

experiences.title: Curated Foça Escape
experiences.text: Boat tour, village route, and gastronomy experience in one package.

faqs.q: What are the check-in / check-out times?
faqs.a: Check-in is from 14:00, and check-out is by 12:00.

faqs.q: Is it suitable for families with children?
faqs.a: Yes, we have family rooms and child-friendly menus available.

faqs.q: How to make an event booking?
faqs.a: Our sales team will contact you within 24 hours of form submission.

Rooms.title: Refined Rooms & Suites
Rooms.text: Discover our accommodations that combine the stone architecture of Kozbeyli Mansion with the peaceful Aegean view.

Footer.description: A refined stop at the heart of Foça, where a 200-year-old story is reinterpreted with luxury and comfort.

Organizations.title: Your Story, Our Venue
Organizations.text: In the heart of the Aegean, we host unforgettable events in the lap of history and nature.
Organizations.contactText: Share the details with us, our expert team will prepare a special offer for you as soon as possible.

packages.title: Boutique Wedding in Registered Heritage
packages.description: Kozbeyli Mansion is a registered building filtered from the common heritage of Greek and Turkish architecture. In our large courtyard with a capacity of up to 150-200 people, we host prestigious weddings where every detail is planned by our 'Expert' team among the faithfully restored stone walls and traditional Horasan mortar craftsmanship.

packages.title: Corporate 'Off-Site' Experience
packages.description: Creative corporate meetings with the thermal comfort of Kozbeyli stone, 15 minutes from Izmir. We combine corporate prestige with history with our boutique meeting capacity for up to 40 people, high-speed internet, and our indoor area with a fireplace.

packages.title: Gourmet Gastronomy Celebrations
packages.description: Crown your special moments such as birthdays or anniversaries with gourmet flavors where Antakya and Aegean cuisines hybridize. Our signature Dibek coffee, hand-pounded in the 180-year-old original stone dibek in the mansion courtyard and slowly cooked over a wood fire, will add a real historical texture to your celebration.

Gastronomy.heroSubtitle: A FLAVOR BRIDGE FROM ANTAKYA TO THE AEGEAN
Gastronomy.inciContent: The kitchen of Kozbeyli Mansion is not just a dining area; it is a 'Gastronomy Laboratory' that combines İnci Hanım's Antakya-based family heritage with the ancient lands of the Aegean.
Gastronomy.dibekContent: Dibek Coffee, pounded fresh every day in the 500-year-old original stone dibek, is a ritual that we are the only real practitioners of in the village.
Gastronomy.breakfastContent: Our spread village breakfast is a completely organic cycle filtered from the ancient olive trees of Kozbeyli and İnci Hanım's traditional jam recipes.

History.textureContent: Kozbeyli Mansion is not just a hotel; it is a 500-year-old registered masterpiece of Ottoman architecture interwoven with Horasan mortar. It is one of the oldest examples of civil architecture in the village.
History.merchantContent: Built between 1870 and 1891 for the wealthiest merchant families of the region, the building reflects the commercial power of the period with its massive wine and olive oil cellars on the ground floor.
History.restorationContent: The restoration, which started in 2012, was completed under the supervision of the Monuments Board, using original techniques without using cement. With the 'Living Museum' philosophy, every stone was preserved by staying true to its original.
